rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF PEIRO CASTAGNOLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/25. Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] No supplement has been filed to indicate who filed the request for special notice on 11/21/97. Notice (incomplete as it was) was not given to the attorney for the former personal representative. If issues are not fully and correctly addressed by 3/4/25, an appearance will be required on 3/18/25. The petition filed on 9/17/24 requested an additional three months to conclude estate administration, which time has clearly already passed.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SVIATOSLAV BOZHENKO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee and proof of the additional bond must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (2) Why did the Social Security benefits and UC pension payments change in 2024 and June 2023, respectively? (3) Does it make sense to pay storage fees for $500 worth of tangible personal property? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/15/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Original bank statements verifying just the ending balance are required for interim accountings. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD CORNELIUS COUGHLIN III
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Department of Tax and Fee Administration is a public entity. [Probate Code Section 9201(a)(2).] (CCP Section 366.2(b)(2) deals with causes of action against a decedent, and the limitations period for commencing an action is not tolled or extended except as provided in (inter alia) Probate Code Section 9000, et seq., meaning after Letters are issued to a general personal representative.) (2) The "updated accounting" does not agree with the I&A, which showed $160,097.00 in cash and $175,000 for the Village Café, or a total of $335,097.00, not $177,427.78. (3) The I&A also did not indicate whether the assets were separate or community property. Please file proof of the characterization of the assets. (4) The administrator acting as an individual does not have legal authority. If she closed the business as the administrator but merely transferred the assets to herself to reopen it individually, she essentially distributed estate assets without a court order. (5) There was also no legal authority for the administrator to pay her attorney or for her attorney to receive fees without a court order. [CRC 7.700(a).] Fees must be returned to the estate. (6) Additionally, the fees paid to Codi Dada exceeded the amount that would have been the statutory fee. Deadline is 4/15/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA M. GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The original will does not appear to have been lodged. (2) If the decedent died on 1/10/17, her brothers, George Garcia and Edward Garcia, appear to have survived her ("prior to 2024" in item 8 is too vague), and her brother Richard A. Garcia certainly survived her since he died on 11/13/24. The heirs of a post-deceased heir/beneficiary are not necessarily the parties interested in the post-deceased heir's/beneficiary's estate. [LRSF 14.18 D.3.] (3) Did all three (presumably) post-deceased brothers die intestate and no estate has been opened for any of them? If an estate was opened or any of them had a trust, the personal representative or successor trustee must be identified and must receive (or waive) notice. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/15/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RON G. VEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another, unless the person entitled to notice has directed in writing that notice be given in care of the other person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] (5) Item 3e(1) of the petition is incomplete. (6) Notice was not published in the "Metropolitan News." (7) If Angela Joy Vest is not entitled to take pursuant Probate Code Section 250, her issue are also not entitled to take. [Probate Code Sect. 250(b)(1).] Please file something to substantiate this allegation. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/15/25. It would be very helpful if a copy of the petition were also provided to the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
